div1.entryFree:
Bare, vb. 1) to strip, to make naked: have --d my bosom to the thunder-stone, Caes. I, 3, 49. that dawning may b. the raven's eye, Cymb. II, 2, 49 (i. e. open; O. Edd. bear).*
2) to shave: shave the head, and tie the beard, and say it was the desire of the penitent to be so --d before his death, Meas. IV, 2, 189. the --ing of my beard, All's IV, 1, 54.
